name: Aminah
shortDescription: Aminah bint Wahb was the mother of the Islamic prophet Muhammad. She is a revered figure in Islam, known for her piety and the extraordinary circumstances surrounding Muhammad's birth.
description: Aminah bint Wahb ibn Abd Manaf ibn Zuhrah ibn Kilab was born into the Banu Zuhrah clan of the Quraysh tribe in Mecca. Her marriage to Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib was arranged, but he died while she was pregnant with Muhammad. Aminah raised Muhammad as a single mother, and he was later sent to live with a Bedouin wet nurse, Halimah bint Abi Dhuayb, according to Arabian custom. When Muhammad was about six years old, Aminah took him to visit his father's relatives in Medina. On their return journey, she fell ill and died in the village of Abwa, near Medina, and was buried there. Aminah is highly regarded in Islamic tradition for her virtuous character and for being the mother of the Prophet Muhammad. Her dream before Muhammad's birth, where she saw a light emanating from her that illuminated the palaces of Syria, is a significant part of Islamic hagiography, interpreted as a sign of Muhammad's future prophethood. Her lineage, belonging to the Banu Zuhrah clan, was also considered noble and contributed to Muhammad's esteemed background. Her story highlights the importance of motherhood and the divine selection in the birth of prophets within the Islamic faith.

sources.title: Sirat Rasul Allah (The Life of Muhammad)
sources.author: Ibn Ishaq

sources.title: The History of al-Tabari, Vol. 6: Muhammad at Mecca
sources.author: Al-Tabari
